content: Framing biological/chemical agents as naturally evolved "triple-signal hybrid retrieval systems" that search for matching target molecules across three distinct signal types: structural shape complementarity, electrostatic binding affinity, and dynamic conformational fit, explains why parallel (rather than sequential) search delivers faster, more accurate molecular recognition just as it improves artificial hybrid retrieval. This parallel natural hybrid retrieval design can be reverse-engineered to build more robust low-latency retrieval systems for RAG.
